quiz.q: Loyalist settlement played a key role in ___.
options: Preventing any settlement in Ontario
Founding many early Ontario communities
Founding cities in Europe
Ending all immigration to Canada

quiz.q: Why did Loyalists choose to leave the newly independent United States?
options: They were forced to leave by the British government
They wished to remain under British rule rather than join the new republic
They had no reason to leave
They were relocating for warmer weather

quiz.q: Studying the Loyalists helps explain ___.
options: Some of the early roots of settlement patterns in what became Ontario
Why Ontario has no early history
That the American Revolution had no effect on Canada
That Ontario was founded in modern times

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Figurative Language: Hyperbole and Onomatopoeia
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Reading strand: hyperbole is an obvious exaggeration used for effect (I have a million things to do), while onomatopoeia is a word that imitates a sound (buzz, crash, sizzle).

quiz.q: Hyperbole is best described as ___.
options: A word that imitates a sound
An obvious exaggeration used for effect
A rhyming pattern
A comparison using like or as

quiz.q: Which sentence contains hyperbole?
options: I finished my homework.
I have three things to do today.
I have a million things to do today.
The sky is blue.

quiz.q: Onomatopoeia is best described as ___.
options: A comparison between two unlike things
A type of punctuation
A word that imitates the sound it describes
An exaggerated statement

quiz.q: Which word is an example of onomatopoeia?
options: Happy
Buzz
Blue
Quickly

quiz.q: Why might an author use hyperbole in writing?
options: Hyperbole has no purpose in writing
To emphasize a feeling or idea in a dramatic, memorable way
To confuse the reader on purpose
To state only literal facts

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Introducing Integers
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Number strand: integers include positive numbers, negative numbers, and zero, and are often used to represent values like temperatures below zero or elevations below sea level.

quiz.q: Which of these is a negative integer?
A temperature of 5 degrees below zero would be written as ___.
On a number line, negative integers are located ___.
options: To the left of zero
To the right of zero
Exactly at zero
Above zero only

quiz.q: Which integer is greater: -8 or -3?
options: They are equal
Cannot be compared

quiz.q: An elevation of 50 metres below sea level could be represented as ___.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Circulatory and Respiratory Systems
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Human Body Systems strand: the circulatory system (heart, blood, and blood vessels) transports oxygen and nutrients through the body, while the respiratory system (lungs and airways) brings oxygen in and removes carbon dioxide.

quiz.q: The main organ of the circulatory system is the ___.
options: Lungs
Heart
Brain
Stomach

quiz.q: The circulatory system’s main job is to ___.
options: Transport oxygen and nutrients throughout the body
Digest food
Filter air only
Control body movement

quiz.q: The main organs of the respiratory system are the ___.
options: Lungs and airways
Stomach and intestines
Heart and blood vessels
Brain and spinal cord

quiz.q: The respiratory system removes ___ from the body.
options: Carbon dioxide
Oxygen
Nutrients
Water only

quiz.q: How do the circulatory and respiratory systems work together?
options: The respiratory system pumps blood
The respiratory system brings in oxygen, which the circulatory system then carries throughout the body
The circulatory system brings in air directly
They have no connection to each other

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Building the Canadian Pacific Railway
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: the Canadian Pacific Railway, completed in 1885, connected Canada from coast to coast, relying heavily on the labour of Chinese immigrant workers who faced dangerous conditions and discrimination.

quiz.q: The Canadian Pacific Railway connected Canada ___.
options: To the United States exclusively
From coast to coast
Nowhere significant
Only within one city

quiz.q: In what decade was the Canadian Pacific Railway completed?
Which group of workers played a major role in building the railway, often under dangerous conditions?
options: Chinese immigrant workers
No workers were needed
Workers from Australia
Only volunteers with no pay

quiz.q: Why is the completion of the railway considered historically significant for Canada?
options: It was built entirely outside of Canada
It had no effect on Canada as a country
It disconnected regions of Canada from each other
It helped unite the country economically and geographically

quiz.q: What is one challenge that Chinese railway workers faced during construction?
options: Easy, safe working conditions with full recognition
Dangerous working conditions and discrimination
No challenges at all
They were paid more than any other workers

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Identifying Theme
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Reading strand: a theme is the underlying message or lesson of a story, often about life, human nature, or society, which is different from the plot itself.

quiz.q: A theme is best described as ___.
options: The exact sequence of events in a story
The name of the main character
The title of the book
The underlying message or lesson of a story

quiz.q: How is theme different from plot?
options: Plot is what happens; theme is the deeper message behind those events
Plot is always about lessons; theme is about events
Theme and plot are exactly the same thing
Theme only exists in nonfiction

quiz.q: Which is an example of a possible theme in a story?
options: The story is 200 pages long.
The book was published last year.
The main character is named Alex.
Perseverance can help you overcome challenges.

quiz.q: How can readers identify a story’s theme?
options: By considering what lesson or message the events of the story suggest
By counting the number of chapters
By reading only the title
By ignoring the characters and plot entirely

quiz.q: Why is identifying theme a valuable reading skill?
options: Only poems have themes
It replaces the need to understand the plot
Themes have no real value to readers
It helps readers understand the deeper meaning and purpose behind a story

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Mean, Median, and Mode
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Data Management strand: the mean is the average of a data set, the median is the middle value when data is ordered, and the mode is the value that appears most often.

quiz.q: What is the mean of the data set 2, 4, 6?
What is the median of the data set 3, 7, 9?
What is the mode of the data set 2, 2, 5, 7?
options: There is no mode

quiz.q: To find the mean, you ___.
options: Find the value that appears most often
Find the middle value only
Multiply all the values together
Add all the values and divide by how many there are

quiz.q: What is the median of the data set 1, 3, 5, 7, 9?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Ecosystems and Biomes of Canada
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Life Systems strand: Canada contains diverse biomes, such as boreal forest, tundra, and grassland, each with distinct plant and animal life adapted to that environment’s climate.

quiz.q: A biome is best described as ___.
options: A single individual animal
A large region with a distinct climate and community of plants and animals
A type of rock
A man-made structure

quiz.q: Canada’s boreal forest biome is characterized mainly by ___.
options: Cactus plants and desert sand
Coral reefs
Coniferous trees and a cold climate
Tropical rainforest plants

quiz.q: The tundra biome is known for its ___.
options: Coral reef ecosystems
Warm, tropical climate
Dense rainforest vegetation
Cold temperatures and lack of trees

quiz.q: Why do different biomes support different plants and animals?
options: Organisms adapt to the specific climate and conditions of their biome
All biomes have identical conditions
Climate has no effect on which organisms live in an area
Plants and animals are randomly distributed with no pattern

quiz.q: Grassland biomes in Canada are typically characterized by ___.
options: Underwater coral formations
Permanent ice cover
Open plains with grasses and few trees
Dense tropical jungle

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Structure of Canadian Courts
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: the Canadian court system includes different levels of courts, from provincial courts to the Supreme Court of Canada, which interpret and apply the law.

quiz.q: The highest court in Canada is the ___.
options: Supreme Court of Canada
Local municipal council
Provincial legislature
House of Commons

quiz.q: The main role of courts in Canada is to ___.
options: Collect taxes
Manage municipal garbage collection
Write new laws from scratch
Interpret and apply the law

quiz.q: Canadian courts are generally organized into ___.
options: A single court with no levels
Different levels, from provincial courts up to the Supreme Court
Courts that only exist in one province
No formal structure at all

quiz.q: Why is having a structured court system important in Canada?
options: Courts have no role in Canadian government
A structured system serves no real purpose
All legal decisions are made by a single person with no appeals
It ensures laws are applied fairly and consistently, with the ability to appeal decisions

quiz.q: A case that is appealed to a higher court is generally being reviewed because ___.
options: Appeals are never allowed in Canada
Someone believes the original decision should be reconsidered
Higher courts only handle unrelated matters
The case has already ended with no further options

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Root Words and Etymology
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Language strand: many English words share root words that come from Latin or Greek, and knowing these roots, along with a word’s etymology (origin), can help readers determine meaning.

quiz.q: A root word is ___.
options: Always a whole sentence
The base part of a word that carries its core meaning
A word’s ending sound only
A punctuation mark

quiz.q: Etymology refers to the study of ___.
options: How to spell a word correctly
A word’s pronunciation only
A word’s origin and history
Grammar rules for verbs

quiz.q: The root word tele- (as in telephone or television) relates to the meaning ___.
options: Water
Small
Fast
Far or distant

quiz.q: Knowing common root words can help readers ___.
options: Ignore unfamiliar words completely
Guess the meaning of unfamiliar words
Change a word’s spelling randomly
Avoid reading new material

quiz.q: Which word shares a root with biology (meaning study of life)?
options: Geography
Biography
Chronology
Astronomy

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Classifying Polyhedra
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Geometry strand: a polyhedron is a 3D shape with flat polygon faces, straight edges, and vertices, such as a cube, pyramid, or prism, classified by the number and shape of their faces.

quiz.q: A polyhedron is best described as a 3D shape with ___.
options: No edges at all
Only curved surfaces
Flat polygon faces, straight edges, and vertices
Only one face

quiz.q: How many faces does a cube have?
A triangular prism has triangular ___ and rectangular side faces.
options: Angles
Bases
Edges
Vertices

quiz.q: A sphere is NOT considered a polyhedron because ___.
options: It is too small
It has no volume
It has curved surfaces, not flat polygon faces
It has flat faces

quiz.q: A square pyramid has how many faces in total, including its base?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Renewable Energy Technologies
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Matter and Energy strand: technologies such as solar panels, wind turbines, and hydroelectric dams convert renewable natural energy sources into usable electricity.

quiz.q: Solar panels convert ___ into usable electricity.
options: Coal
Wind energy
Sunlight
Ocean waves only

quiz.q: Wind turbines convert ___ into usable electricity.
options: Underground heat only
The motion of wind
Coal
Sunlight

quiz.q: Hydroelectric dams generate electricity using the movement of ___.
options: Sunlight
Water
Wind
Coal

quiz.q: Why are renewable energy technologies an important area of research?
options: Renewable technologies cannot generate any electricity
They can generate electricity while reducing reliance on non-renewable resources
They increase pollution significantly
They have no benefits at all

quiz.q: Which of these best fits the category of renewable energy technology?
options: A natural gas furnace
A coal power plant
A wind turbine
An oil refinery

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Indigenous Self-Government and Modern Treaties
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: many Indigenous communities in Canada today exercise self-government through modern treaties and agreements that recognize their right to govern their own affairs.

quiz.q: Self-government for Indigenous communities generally means ___.
options: Having no say in their own affairs
Being governed entirely by another country
Having no recognized rights at all
Having the authority to govern their own community affairs

quiz.q: A modern treaty between a government and an Indigenous community typically addresses ___.
options: Only sports agreements
Rights, land, and governance arrangements
Only trade with other countries
Nothing of importance

quiz.q: Why is self-government important to many Indigenous communities?
options: It has no connection to community wellbeing
Self-government has no real impact on communities
It removes all rights from Indigenous peoples
It allows communities to make decisions that reflect their own needs, values, and traditions

quiz.q: Modern treaties differ from historical treaties in that they often ___.
options: Are identical to treaties signed centuries ago
Have no legal significance at all
Cannot involve Indigenous communities in negotiations
Are negotiated to address more contemporary rights and governance issues

quiz.q: Learning about Indigenous self-government helps students understand ___.
options: That treaties are no longer relevant
That Indigenous communities have no role in modern Canada
How Indigenous nations exercise rights and responsibilities today
That Indigenous governance ended in the past

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Research Report Writing
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Writing strand: a research report organizes information gathered from multiple credible sources into a clear structure, typically with an introduction, body paragraphs by subtopic, and a conclusion.

quiz.q: A research report typically begins with ___.
options: An introduction that presents the topic
A random unrelated fact
The conclusion
Only a list of sources

quiz.q: Body paragraphs in a research report are usually organized by ___.
options: The length of each sentence
Random order with no structure
Alphabetical order of unrelated words
Subtopic

quiz.q: Why is it important to use multiple credible sources in a research report?
options: Using more than one source always causes confusion
Multiple sources are never necessary
Credibility of sources does not matter
To gather accurate, well-supported information on the topic

quiz.q: A research report’s conclusion should ___.
options: Introduce a completely new topic
Repeat the introduction word for word
Summarize the key findings from the report
Contain no information at all

quiz.q: Why should a research report cite its sources?
options: It makes the report less trustworthy
To give credit and allow readers to verify the information
Citing sources is unnecessary
Sources should always be hidden from the reader

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Solving Simple Equations
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Algebra strand: a simple equation with a missing value can be solved by using the inverse (opposite) operation to isolate the unknown, such as solving x + 5 = 12 by subtracting 5 from both sides.

quiz.q: Solve for x: x + 5 = 12.
Solve for y: y minus 4 = 10.
To solve x + 5 = 12, which operation should you use to isolate x?
options: Add 5 to both sides
Multiply both sides by 5
Divide both sides by 5
Subtract 5 from both sides

quiz.q: Solve for n: 3 times n = 21.
Solve for m: m divided by 4 = 6.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Space: The Solar System and Beyond
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: the solar system consists of the Sun and everything that orbits it, including eight planets, moons, and other bodies like asteroids and comets, within the much larger Milky Way galaxy.

quiz.q: The Sun is best described as a ___.
options: Moon
Star
Comet
Planet

quiz.q: How many planets are in our solar system?
options: Eight
Seven
Ten
Nine

quiz.q: Our solar system is located within the ___ galaxy.
options: Milky Way
Andromeda
Triangulum
Whirlpool

quiz.q: Which of these is a small icy body that orbits the Sun and can develop a visible tail?
options: A star
A moon
A planet
A comet

quiz.q: Why is the Sun considered the centre of our solar system?
options: The Sun has no connection to the other planets
The Sun orbits around Earth
Its gravity holds the planets and other bodies in orbit around it
The Sun is the smallest object in the solar system

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Role in the World Wars
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: Canada played a significant role in both World War I and World War II, with Canadian soldiers contributing to major battles and the country’s involvement shaping its national identity.

quiz.q: Canada was involved in which two major global conflicts of the 20th century?
options: World War I and World War II
Only local skirmishes with no global conflicts
The War of 1812 twice
Conflicts that never happened

quiz.q: Canadian soldiers are remembered for their contributions to major battles during ___.
options: The dinosaur era
A period with no recorded history
The World Wars
Ancient Roman times

quiz.q: Canada’s involvement in the World Wars is often seen as having helped ___.
options: Erase Canada’s identity completely
Have no impact on Canada at all
Prevent Canada from ever having an army
Shape a stronger sense of national identity

quiz.q: Why do Canadians observe Remembrance Day?
options: It has no connection to Canadian history
To honour those who served and sacrificed during wartime
To celebrate a modern holiday unrelated to history
To mark the founding of a new country

quiz.q: Learning about Canada’s role in the World Wars helps students understand ___.
options: That Canada was not involved in any global conflicts
How historical events shaped Canada’s development and identity
That history has no connection to identity
That these wars had no effect on Canada

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Media Literacy: Analyzing Advertisements
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Media Literacy strand: analyzing advertisements involves identifying the intended audience, the persuasive techniques used, and the underlying message the advertiser wants viewers to believe.

quiz.q: When analyzing an advertisement, it is useful to consider ___.
options: The intended audience and persuasive techniques used
Nothing beyond the product name
Only the colours used
The length of the advertisement only

quiz.q: The intended audience of an advertisement refers to ___.
options: Random unrelated viewers only
The advertiser’s competitors
The group of people the ad is designed to appeal to
People who dislike the product

quiz.q: Which is a common persuasive technique used in advertisements?
options: Presenting only balanced, neutral information
Refusing to mention the product at all
Avoiding any attempt to persuade the viewer
Using emotional appeals or catchy slogans

quiz.q: Why is it useful for viewers to analyze advertisements critically?
options: To understand how they are being persuaded and make informed choices
All advertisements present entirely objective information
Analyzing ads is only useful for advertisers
Critical analysis of ads serves no purpose

quiz.q: An advertisement’s underlying message is ___.
options: What the advertiser ultimately wants the viewer to believe or do
Irrelevant to understanding the ad
The same in every advertisement
Always stated directly in plain text

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Converting Units of Measurement
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Measurement strand: converting between metric units, such as centimetres to metres or grams to kilograms, involves multiplying or dividing by powers of ten based on the relationship between the units.

quiz.q: How many centimetres are in 1 metre?
How many grams are in 1 kilogram?
Convert 250 centimetres to metres.
Convert 3 kilograms to grams.
To convert a smaller unit to a larger unit, you typically ___.
options: Divide
Always subtract 10
Always add 10
Multiply

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Mixtures and Solutions
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Matter and Energy strand: a mixture combines two or more substances that keep their own properties, while a solution is a special type of mixture where one substance dissolves evenly into another.

quiz.q: A mixture is formed when ___.
options: Nothing is combined at all
A substance disappears completely
Two or more substances combine but keep their own properties
A single substance changes into a different element

quiz.q: A solution is a type of mixture where ___.
options: All substances turn into gas
One substance dissolves evenly into another
The mixture always separates immediately
Substances never combine

quiz.q: Which is an example of a solution?
options: Rocks mixed with soil
Salt dissolved in water
Oil floating on water
Sand mixed with water

quiz.q: Which is an example of a mixture that is NOT a solution?
options: Salt dissolved in water
Food colouring mixed into water
Sugar dissolved in tea
Sand and gravel mixed together

quiz.q: Why might a solution be described as evenly distributed?
options: Solutions cannot be mixed evenly
The dissolved substance spreads uniformly throughout the other substance
Solutions always separate into visible layers
Solutions contain only one substance

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Economic Systems: Needs, Wants, and Resources
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: economic systems involve decisions about how to use limited resources to meet unlimited needs and wants, balancing production, distribution, and consumption of goods and services.

quiz.q: A need is best described as ___.
options: Something essential for survival, like food or shelter
Something with no importance
Something entirely optional
The same thing as a want

quiz.q: A want is best described as ___.
options: The exact same as a need
Something desired but not essential for survival
Something that never affects decisions
Something essential for survival

quiz.q: Economic systems must make decisions about resources because resources are generally ___.
options: Unlimited
Limited
Always identical everywhere
Irrelevant to decision-making

quiz.q: Which is an example of a resource used in producing goods?
options: Raw materials, like wood or metal
Only money, with no other resources
Nothing is needed to produce goods
Only imagination, with no physical resources

quiz.q: Why must societies make choices about how to use limited resources?
options: Resources are always unlimited everywhere
Choices about resources are never necessary
Because needs and wants can exceed what resources are available
Every society has identical, unlimited resources

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Complex and Compound Sentences
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Writing strand: a compound sentence joins two independent clauses with a conjunction (I like reading, and I like writing), while a complex sentence joins an independent clause with a dependent clause (Although it was raining, we went outside).

quiz.q: A compound sentence joins two ___ with a conjunction.
options: Independent clauses
Punctuation marks
Single words
Dependent clauses

quiz.q: A complex sentence joins an independent clause with a ___ clause.
options: Dependent
Punctuation-only
Meaningless
Second independent

quiz.q: Which sentence is a compound sentence?
options: Although it was raining, we went outside.
Reading is fun because it is relaxing.
I like reading, and I like writing.
I like reading.

quiz.q: Which sentence is a complex sentence?
options: Although it was raining, we went outside.
I like reading, and I like writing.
I read books.
The dog barked.

quiz.q: A dependent clause is a clause that ___.
options: Is always the entire sentence
Never appears in complex sentences
Can stand alone as a complete sentence
Cannot stand alone as a complete sentence

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Multi-Step Word Problems with Percent and Fractions
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Number strand: solving multi-step word problems involving percent and fractions requires identifying each step needed, such as first finding a percentage of a number, then adding or subtracting the result.

quiz.q: A $40 shirt is 25 percent off. What is the discount amount?
A $40 shirt is 25 percent off. What is the final sale price?
Maria read 1/4 of a 200-page book on Monday, then 1/2 of the remaining pages on Tuesday. How many pages did she read on Tuesday?
Solving a multi-step problem with percent and fractions requires you to ___.
options: Use only one operation regardless of the problem
Ignore the given information
Skip steps to save time
Complete each necessary step in order to reach the final answer

quiz.q: A class of 30 students has 60 percent who prefer math. How many students prefer math?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Erosion, Weathering, and Landform Formation
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: weathering breaks rock down into smaller pieces, while erosion moves those pieces elsewhere via wind, water, or ice, together gradually shaping landforms over time.

quiz.q: Weathering is the process of ___.
options: Breaking rock down into smaller pieces
Creating brand new mountains instantly
Moving broken rock pieces to a new location
Freezing water into ice permanently

quiz.q: Erosion is the process of ___.
options: Moving broken-down material to a new location
Preventing any landform changes
Only affecting rocks underground
Breaking rock into pieces only

quiz.q: Which of these can cause both weathering and erosion?
options: Only artificial materials
Only complete stillness
Nothing natural causes these processes
Wind, water, and ice

quiz.q: Over long periods of time, weathering and erosion together can ___.
options: Instantly create new landforms in seconds
Gradually shape landforms like valleys and canyons
Have no effect on the land
Only occur underwater

quiz.q: Why might a canyon take thousands of years to form?
options: Canyons cannot form through natural processes
Weathering and erosion typically occur gradually rather than instantly
These processes have no connection to landform formation
Canyons are always formed in a single day

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Political Parties and How Laws Are Made
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: political parties represent different ideas about how the country should be run, and elected representatives from these parties debate and vote to turn proposed bills into laws.

quiz.q: A political party is best described as a group that ___.
options: Has no political views at all
Only exists at the municipal level
Never participates in elections
Shares similar ideas about how the country should be governed

quiz.q: A proposed law before it is passed is called a ___.
options: Bylaw
Amendment only
Bill
Treaty

quiz.q: For a bill to become law in Canada, it generally must be ___.
options: Ignored completely by all representatives
Written by a single citizen with no review
Debated and voted on by elected representatives
Approved without any discussion

quiz.q: Why do different political parties often have different positions on issues?
options: Parties are randomly assigned positions with no reasoning
Political parties have no real differences
All political parties always agree on everything
They represent different values, priorities, and approaches to governing

quiz.q: Why is it useful for citizens to understand how laws are made?
options: This knowledge has no value to citizens
Laws are made with no citizen relevance
It helps them understand and participate in the democratic process
Only elected officials need to understand this process

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Analyzing Character Development
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Reading strand: character development is how a character changes or grows over the course of a story, often shown through their actions, decisions, and relationships with others.

quiz.q: Character development refers to ___.
options: How a character changes or grows over a story
The book’s cover illustration
The exact number of characters in a book
The font used to print the book

quiz.q: Which is a way authors often reveal character development?
options: Through a character’s actions and decisions over time
Through the page numbers
Through the price listed on the book
Only through the table of contents

quiz.q: A character who learns an important lesson by the end of a story shows an example of ___.
options: A change in the book’s title
No development at all
A change in the setting only
Character development

quiz.q: Why do readers pay attention to character development?
options: It is not something authors intentionally include
Only the plot matters, never the characters
It deepens understanding of the story’s meaning and the character’s journey
Character development has no connection to a story’s meaning

quiz.q: Which detail would best show a character’s growth in courage?
options: A description of the character’s house
The character finally standing up for a friend despite feeling afraid
The character’s favourite food
The time of day the story takes place

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Simple Interest and Saving
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Financial Literacy strand: simple interest is money earned or paid based on a percentage of an original amount saved or borrowed, calculated using the interest rate and the amount of time involved.

quiz.q: If you save $100 at a 5 percent simple interest rate for one year, how much interest do you earn?
Simple interest is generally calculated based on ___.
options: A percentage of the original amount saved or borrowed
Nothing related to the amount saved
The colour of the bank card used
A fixed dollar amount unrelated to percentage

quiz.q: Why might someone choose to save money in an account that earns interest?
options: Interest is only relevant to borrowing, never saving
Their savings can grow over time through earned interest
Savings accounts always lose money
Interest never adds any value to savings

quiz.q: If you save $200 at a 10 percent simple interest rate for one year, what is your total after one year?
Interest paid on borrowed money is generally an amount ___.
options: Subtracted from the amount borrowed
Always equal to zero
Owed in addition to the original amount borrowed
Unrelated to the amount borrowed

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Scientific Method and Experimental Design
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Inquiry strand: the scientific method involves asking a question, forming a hypothesis, testing it through a fair experiment, and analyzing results to draw a conclusion.

quiz.q: The scientific method typically begins with ___.
options: Drawing a final conclusion
Building a finished product
Publishing results
Asking a question

quiz.q: A hypothesis is best described as ___.
options: A proven fact that cannot be tested
The final step of an experiment
A random guess with no reasoning at all
A testable prediction about what might happen

quiz.q: Why do scientists analyze their results after an experiment?
options: Only the experiment’s setup matters, not the results
Analysis of results is unnecessary
To determine whether the results support or refute the hypothesis
Results are always ignored in science

quiz.q: A conclusion in the scientific method should be based on ___.
options: What the scientist wished would happen
Personal opinion with no evidence
The evidence gathered during the experiment
Random chance only

quiz.q: Why is the scientific method useful across many areas of science?
options: It only applies to one specific science topic
It provides a structured, reliable way to investigate questions and test ideas
It replaces the need for any evidence
It has no practical use in science

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s National Parks and Protected Areas
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: Canada’s national parks and protected areas conserve important natural environments and wildlife habitats, balancing conservation with public access and recreation.

quiz.q: The main purpose of a national park is to ___.
options: Remove all wildlife from the area
Conserve natural environments and wildlife habitats
Build as many buildings as possible
Prevent any public access forever

quiz.q: Which organization manages many of Canada’s national parks?
options: A private international company
A foreign government
Parks Canada
No organization manages them

quiz.q: Protected areas help ___.
options: Eliminate biodiversity entirely
Increase pollution in natural areas
Encourage unlimited resource extraction
Preserve biodiversity and natural habitats

quiz.q: Why might a national park allow limited public access, like hiking trails?
options: Public access has no connection to conservation goals
To let people appreciate and connect with nature while minimizing environmental impact
National parks never allow any visitors
Public access always destroys protected areas completely

quiz.q: Why are national parks and protected areas important to Canada?
options: They exist only to generate profit with no other purpose
They serve no meaningful purpose
They help conserve natural heritage for future generations
They have no connection to environmental conservation

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Analyzing Setting and Mood
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Reading strand: setting is the time and place of a story, and mood is the feeling or atmosphere the setting and word choice create for the reader.

quiz.q: Setting refers to ___.
options: The book’s title
The number of chapters
The main character’s name
The time and place of a story

quiz.q: Mood refers to ___.
options: The feeling or atmosphere created for the reader
The exact page count
The font used in the book
The author’s home address

quiz.q: A dark, stormy setting with creaking floors would likely create a ___ mood.
options: Silly
Relaxed
Tense or eerie
Cheerful

quiz.q: Which technique might an author use to build a peaceful mood?
options: Describing a quiet meadow with soft sunlight
Listing random unrelated facts
Describing a loud, chaotic battle
Using only dialogue with no description

quiz.q: Why is setting closely connected to mood?
options: Setting and mood are always unrelated
Setting has no effect on how a reader feels
Mood only comes from character names
Details about time and place often shape the emotional atmosphere of a scene

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Unit Rates
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Number strand: a unit rate compares a quantity to one unit of another quantity, such as kilometres per hour or price per item, making it easier to compare different options.

quiz.q: If a car travels 300 kilometres in 5 hours, what is its unit rate in kilometres per hour?
options: 50 km/h
60 km/h
5 km/h

quiz.q: A unit rate compares a quantity to ___.
options: A completely unrelated quantity
Nothing at all
Ten units of another quantity
One unit of another quantity

quiz.q: If 4 apples cost $2, what is the unit rate (price per apple)?
Unit rates are useful for ___.
options: Making comparisons impossible
Comparing which option offers better value
Ignoring price differences
Only measuring time

quiz.q: If a runner covers 100 metres in 20 seconds, what is their unit rate in metres per second?
options: 5 m/s
80 m/s
20 m/s

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Skeletal and Muscular Systems
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Human Body Systems strand: the skeletal system (bones) provides structure and protection for the body, while the muscular system works with bones to allow movement.

quiz.q: The main role of the skeletal system is to ___.
options: Filter air
Digest food
Provide structure and protect internal organs
Pump blood

quiz.q: The muscular system works with the skeletal system to ___.
options: Digest food only
Allow the body to move
Filter blood
Control emotions directly

quiz.q: Which body part is protected by the ribcage, part of the skeletal system?
options: Fingernails
Heart and lungs
Hair
Skin only

quiz.q: Muscles typically move the body by ___.
options: Dissolving completely
Contracting and relaxing
Filtering waste
Producing digestive enzymes

quiz.q: Why do the skeletal and muscular systems need to work together?
options: Muscles have no connection to bones
Muscles pull on bones to create movement at the joints
Bones move entirely on their own without muscles
They function completely independently with no connection

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Great Depression in Canada
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: the Great Depression of the 1930s caused widespread unemployment and hardship across Canada, leading to significant changes in government support programs.

quiz.q: The Great Depression took place mainly during which decade?
The Great Depression caused widespread ___.
options: Economic prosperity
Unemployment and hardship
Immediate improvement in living conditions
Population growth with no problems

quiz.q: The hardships of the Great Depression contributed to changes in ___.
options: Foreign languages spoken in Canada
Only sports rules
Government support programs
Nothing related to government policy

quiz.q: Why is the Great Depression significant to Canadian history?
options: It only affected other countries, not Canada
It happened in modern times
It had no lasting impact on Canada
It caused major economic and social hardship that shaped later government policy

quiz.q: Which is an example of hardship many Canadians faced during the Great Depression?
options: No economic changes at all
Unlimited access to jobs and wealth
Widespread job loss and poverty
A booming economy with no challenges

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Modifiers and Clarity
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Writing strand: a modifier is a word or phrase that describes another word, and misplaced modifiers can make a sentence unclear or confusing if placed too far from the word they describe.

quiz.q: A modifier is a word or phrase that ___.
options: Replaces punctuation
Describes another word in the sentence
Is always the subject of a sentence
Has no grammatical function

quiz.q: A misplaced modifier can cause a sentence to ___.
options: Become clearer
Become confusing or unclear
Automatically fix itself
Have no effect on meaning

quiz.q: Which sentence has a misplaced modifier? Running quickly, the finish line was reached by Sam.
options: This sentence
Sam ran quickly to the finish line.
Sam reached the finish line.
The finish line was near Sam.

quiz.q: Why is it important to place modifiers close to the word they describe?
options: Clarity is not a goal of good writing
Placement of modifiers never matters
Modifiers should always be at the very end of a paragraph
To avoid confusing or unintended meanings in a sentence

quiz.q: Which is a corrected, clear version of a modifier issue? Wearing a red hat, I saw my friend.
options: Both sentences mean the exact same thing with no ambiguity.
Wearing a red hat, my friend I saw.
Neither sentence uses a modifier.
I saw my friend wearing a red hat.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Multiplying Fractions by Fractions
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Number strand: to multiply two fractions, multiply the numerators together and the denominators together, then simplify the result if possible.

quiz.q: What is 1/2 times 1/3?
What is 2/3 times 3/4?
options: 6/12, which simplifies to 1/2

quiz.q: To multiply two fractions, you ___.
options: Only multiply the numerators
Multiply the numerators and multiply the denominators
Add the numerators and denominators
Only multiply the denominators

quiz.q: What is 3/5 times 1/2?
What is 2/4 times 2/4?
options: 4/16, which simplifies to 1/4

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: The Excretory System
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Human Body Systems strand: the excretory system, including the kidneys and bladder, removes waste products and excess water from the blood to keep the body’s internal environment balanced.

quiz.q: The main organs of the excretory system include the ___.
options: Heart and lungs
Brain and spinal cord
Stomach and intestines only
Kidneys and bladder

quiz.q: The main role of the excretory system is to ___.
options: Pump oxygen throughout the body
Digest food completely
Control body temperature only
Remove waste products and excess water from the blood

quiz.q: The kidneys filter waste out of the ___.
options: Blood
Bones
Muscles
Skin

quiz.q: Why is the excretory system important for overall health?
options: It only affects hair growth
It helps maintain a balanced internal environment by removing waste
It has no real role in the body
It produces energy for muscles

quiz.q: Urine, produced by the kidneys, is eventually stored in the ___ before being removed from the body.
options: Bladder
Heart
Stomach
Lungs

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Path to Full Independence: The Statute of Westminster
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: the Statute of Westminster, passed in 1931, gave Canada and other Commonwealth countries greater legal independence from Britain in making their own laws.

quiz.q: The Statute of Westminster was passed in which year?
The Statute of Westminster gave Canada greater ___.
options: Legal independence in making its own laws
Control over other countries
Isolation from the Commonwealth
Dependence on Britain

quiz.q: Before the Statute of Westminster, Canada’s laws were more closely tied to ___.
options: The United States
No other country
France
Britain

quiz.q: Why is the Statute of Westminster considered an important step in Canadian history?
options: It made Canada less independent
It ended Canada’s relationship with the Commonwealth entirely
It had no effect on Canada’s legal status
It marked significant progress toward Canada governing itself independently

quiz.q: The Statute of Westminster applied to Canada and other ___ countries.
options: Unrelated
South American
Commonwealth
European Union

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Writing a News Report
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Writing strand: a news report presents factual information about a recent event, typically answering who, what, when, where, why, and how, often starting with the most important details.

quiz.q: A news report typically answers questions including ___.
options: Who, what, when, where, why, and how
Nothing specific at all
Only the writer’s opinion
Only the weather

quiz.q: News reports usually begin with ___.
options: The least important details
A poem
The most important details
A long unrelated introduction

quiz.q: Why should a news report focus on factual information?
options: To accurately inform readers about what happened
Opinions should replace facts entirely
News reports should always be fictional
Facts are not important in news reports

quiz.q: Which is an example of a strong news report opening?
options: A fire broke out at a downtown building early this morning, displacing twelve residents.
Once upon a time, in a faraway land...
This report has no clear topic.
Fires are sometimes hot.

quiz.q: Why is it useful for a news report to include where an event happened?
options: Where something happened has no value to readers
News reports should never mention places
Location is never relevant to news
It helps readers understand the specific context and location of the event

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Volume of Triangular Prisms
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Geometry strand: the volume of a triangular prism can be found by multiplying the area of its triangular base by the length (height) of the prism.

quiz.q: The formula for the volume of a triangular prism is ___.
options: Base area times length of the prism
Base area divided by length
Length times width only
Perimeter times height

quiz.q: If a triangular prism’s base area is 12 square units and its length is 5 units, what is its volume?
options: 30 cubic units
17 cubic units
60 cubic units
12 cubic units

quiz.q: To find a triangular prism’s volume, you first need to know its ___.
options: Base area and length
Number of edges only
Surface temperature
Colour and weight

quiz.q: If a triangular prism’s base area is 8 square units and its length is 10 units, what is its volume?
options: 800 cubic units
18 cubic units
80 cubic units
8 cubic units

quiz.q: Volume is measured in ___ units.
options: Square
Degree
Linear
Cubic

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Compound Machines: Combining Simple Machines
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Structures and Mechanisms strand: a compound machine combines two or more simple machines, such as a wheelbarrow (lever and wheel-and-axle) or scissors (two levers), to make tasks easier.

quiz.q: A compound machine is made up of ___.
options: Two or more simple machines working together
Only electrical parts
Only one simple machine
No simple machines at all

quiz.q: A wheelbarrow combines which two simple machines?
options: A lever and a wheel-and-axle
Two screws
A pulley and a screw
Two inclined planes

quiz.q: Scissors are an example of a compound machine made from ___.
options: A single inclined plane
A pulley and a wheel
Two screws
Two levers

quiz.q: Why might a compound machine be more useful than a single simple machine?
options: Simple machines can never be combined
Compound machines are always less useful than simple ones
Combining simple machines can accomplish more complex tasks efficiently
Compound machines cannot perform any task

quiz.q: Which everyday tool is an example of a compound machine?
options: A flat ramp with nothing else
A pair of scissors
A single nail
A plain rope

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The 1982 Constitution Act and the Charter of Rights and Freedoms
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: the 1982 Constitution Act patriated Canada’s constitution from Britain and included the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms, which protects fundamental rights of Canadians.

quiz.q: The Constitution Act was passed in which year?
options: 1867 and 1931 only

quiz.q: Patriating the constitution meant Canada could ___.
options: No longer amend its own constitution
Amend its own constitution without approval from Britain
Give up its constitution entirely
Only follow British law forever

quiz.q: The Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms is included within the ___.
options: Statute of Westminster
1867 British North America Act
1982 Constitution Act
A separate, unrelated document

quiz.q: The Charter of Rights and Freedoms is meant to protect ___.
options: Only the rights of elected officials
Fundamental rights of people in Canada
Only property rights
No one’s rights

quiz.q: Why is 1982 considered an important year in Canadian constitutional history?
options: Nothing significant happened that year
Canada gained full control over its constitution and adopted the Charter
Canada joined a new country that year
Canada lost its independence that year

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Figurative Language: Symbolism
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Reading strand: symbolism is when an object, character, or colour represents a deeper idea or meaning beyond its literal sense, such as a dove symbolizing peace.

quiz.q: Symbolism is when something represents ___.
options: Only its literal, surface meaning
Nothing at all
A deeper idea or meaning beyond its literal sense
A random unrelated word

quiz.q: A dove is commonly used as a symbol of ___.
options: Confusion
War
Peace
Anger

quiz.q: Why do authors use symbolism in their writing?
options: To make stories completely literal with no deeper meaning
Symbolism has no purpose in writing
To confuse readers with no intended meaning
To add deeper meaning and layers of interpretation to a story

quiz.q: Which could serve as a symbol of new beginnings in a story?
options: A sunrise
An empty page with nothing described
A broken clock with no context
A random number

quiz.q: Symbolism is different from a literal description because it ___.
options: Suggests meaning beyond the object’s surface appearance
Only describes exactly what something looks like
Never appears in stories
Is the same as describing a character’s height

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Circles: Radius, Diameter, and Circumference
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Geometry strand: a circle’s radius is the distance from the centre to the edge, its diameter is twice the radius, and its circumference is the distance around the circle.

quiz.q: The radius of a circle is the distance from ___.
options: One edge to the opposite edge
The circumference to the diameter
Nowhere in particular
The centre to the edge

quiz.q: The diameter of a circle is ___ the radius.
options: Twice
Half of
Equal to
Four times

quiz.q: If a circle’s radius is 5 cm, what is its diameter?
options: 5 cm
10 cm
15 cm
2.5 cm

quiz.q: The circumference of a circle refers to ___.
options: The radius only
The centre point
The area inside the circle
The distance around the circle

quiz.q: If a circle’s diameter is 8 cm, what is its radius?
options: 8 cm
2 cm
16 cm
4 cm

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Properties of Air and Atmosphere
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Matter and Energy strand: air is a mixture of gases that makes up Earth’s atmosphere, has mass and takes up space, and plays a key role in weather and supporting life.

quiz.q: Air is best described as ___.
options: A solid material
A mixture of gases
A single pure element
Empty space with nothing in it

quiz.q: Which of these is true about air?
options: It has no mass and takes up no space
It has mass and takes up space
It has no connection to weather
It cannot move at all

quiz.q: Earth’s atmosphere plays an important role in ___.
options: Nothing important
Supporting life and influencing weather
Preventing all forms of weather
Blocking all forms of life

quiz.q: Which gas makes up the largest portion of Earth’s atmosphere?
options: Nitrogen
Oxygen
Carbon dioxide
Hydrogen

quiz.q: Why is it important to study the properties of air and atmosphere?
options: Air properties never change
The atmosphere has no connection to weather
It helps us understand weather patterns and the conditions that support life
Air has no scientific importance

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Regional Economic Differences Across Canada
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: different regions of Canada have distinct economies based on their available resources and industries, such as fishing in Atlantic Canada or oil production in Alberta.

quiz.q: Atlantic Canada’s economy has historically relied heavily on ___.
options: Coral reef tourism only
Desert farming
Fishing
Oil production

quiz.q: Alberta’s economy has historically relied heavily on ___.
options: Deep-sea fishing
Rainforest logging
Oil production
Nothing at all

quiz.q: Regional economic differences across Canada are often shaped by ___.
options: Identical factors everywhere in Canada
Random chance with no connection to resources
Nothing related to geography
Available natural resources and local industries

quiz.q: Why might different provinces specialize in different economic activities?
options: Economic activity is unrelated to geography
Specialization has no connection to available resources
They make use of the resources and conditions unique to their region
All provinces have exactly the same resources

quiz.q: Which is an example of a resource-based industry in a specific Canadian region?
options: Forestry in British Columbia
Volcano tourism in the Prairies
Desert farming in the Arctic
Coral reef diving in Ontario

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Distinguishing Facts from Inferences
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Reading strand: a fact is directly stated in a text, while an inference is a logical conclusion a reader draws by combining text clues with their own knowledge.

quiz.q: A fact in a text is ___.
options: Always a guess with no evidence
Never found in nonfiction
Directly stated information
The same thing as an inference

quiz.q: An inference is ___.
options: Impossible to support with evidence
Always incorrect
Directly stated in the text word for word
A logical conclusion drawn from text clues and prior knowledge

quiz.q: If a text says a character’s hands were shaking as they opened a letter, a reasonable inference might be that the character felt ___.
options: Nervous or anxious
Extremely relaxed
Nothing at all
Bored

quiz.q: Why is it useful for readers to make inferences?
options: Only facts matter when reading a text
It helps readers understand ideas that are implied but not directly stated
Inferences should always be avoided
Inferences are never useful in reading

quiz.q: An inference is considered reasonable when it is ___.
options: Unrelated to anything in the text
Based on clues from the text and logical thinking
A completely random guess
Always identical to a stated fact

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Constructing and Interpreting Circle Graphs
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Data Management strand: a circle graph (pie chart) shows data as slices of a circle, where each slice’s size represents its proportion of the whole set of data.

quiz.q: In a circle graph, the size of each slice represents ___.
options: Its proportion of the whole data set
Nothing measurable
The colour used
A random unrelated value

quiz.q: A circle graph is also commonly known as a ___.
options: Pie chart
Line graph
Pictograph
Bar graph

quiz.q: If half of a circle graph is shaded for one category, that category represents ___ of the total data.
options: 10 percent
25 percent
100 percent
50 percent

quiz.q: Circle graphs are especially useful for showing ___.
options: Data over time only
Only exact numerical values with no proportions
Data with no relationship to a total
How parts relate to a whole

quiz.q: If a circle graph has four equal slices, each slice represents ___ of the total.
options: 25 percent
20 percent
10 percent
50 percent

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Stars and Constellations
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: stars are massive, glowing balls of gas that produce their own light and heat, and patterns of stars form constellations that have been used historically for navigation.

quiz.q: A star is best described as ___.
options: A massive, glowing ball of gas that produces its own light and heat
A small rocky planet
A moon orbiting a planet
A type of comet

quiz.q: A constellation is ___.
options: A recognized pattern formed by a group of stars
A type of planet
A single very bright star
A type of galaxy

quiz.q: Historically, constellations have been used for ___.
options: Farming schedules only
Building houses
Navigation and storytelling
Cooking recipes

quiz.q: Why do stars appear as small points of light from Earth?
options: They are extremely far away, despite being enormous in size
Stars are actually very small in real life
Stars produce no light at all
Stars are the same size as planets

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a well-known constellation?
options: The Big Dipper
The Sahara Desert
The Rocky Mountains
The Great Lakes

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Urbanization and Its Effects on Canadian Cities
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: urbanization is the growth of cities as more people move from rural areas to urban centres, which can affect housing, transportation, and the environment.

quiz.q: Urbanization refers to ___.
options: A decrease in city populations
The growth of cities as populations shift toward urban centres
The disappearance of all cities
Nothing related to population movement

quiz.q: Which challenge is commonly associated with rapid urbanization?
options: Increased demand for housing and transportation
A complete lack of any challenges
Fewer people needing services
No effect on infrastructure at all

quiz.q: Urbanization can affect the environment by ___.
options: Reducing the need for any resources
Automatically improving air quality with no effort
Having no environmental impact whatsoever
Increasing land development and resource use in cities

quiz.q: Why might people move from rural areas to cities?
options: Rural areas always have more job opportunities
Cities never offer any advantages
People never choose to move for economic reasons
For access to jobs, services, and opportunities often concentrated in urban areas

quiz.q: City planners must consider urbanization’s effects on ___.
options: Weather patterns exclusively
Nothing related to daily life
Only the colour of buildings
Housing, transportation, and environmental sustainability

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Connotation and Denotation
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Language strand: denotation is a word’s literal dictionary definition, while connotation is the feeling or association a word carries beyond its literal meaning, such as childish versus youthful.

quiz.q: Denotation refers to ___.
options: A word’s spelling only
The feeling a word suggests
A word’s pronunciation only
A word’s literal dictionary definition

quiz.q: Connotation refers to ___.
options: A word’s literal definition only
A word’s part of speech
The feeling or association a word carries beyond its literal meaning
A word’s syllable count

quiz.q: The words childish and youthful have similar denotations but different ___.
options: Pronunciations
Definitions in the dictionary
Spellings
Connotations

quiz.q: Which word likely has a more positive connotation than stubborn, despite a similar meaning?
options: Careless
Rude
Determined
Lazy

quiz.q: Why is understanding connotation useful for writers?
options: Connotation only applies to poetry
Writers should always ignore how words feel to readers
Connotation has no effect on writing
It helps them choose words that create the intended tone or feeling

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Geometric Nets and 3D Shape Construction
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Geometry strand: a net is a 2D pattern that can be folded to form a 3D shape, showing all of the shape’s faces laid out flat.

quiz.q: A net is best described as ___.
options: A measurement of angles only
A type of fraction
A type of graph
A 2D pattern that folds into a 3D shape

quiz.q: The net of a cube is made up of how many square faces?
Nets help us understand 3D shapes by ___.
options: Turning a 3D shape into a single point
Showing all of a shape’s faces laid out flat
Removing the need for any faces
Hiding all of a shape’s faces

quiz.q: The net of a triangular prism includes ___.
options: Two triangles and three rectangles
One single triangle only
Only circles
Six squares

quiz.q: Why might builders or designers use nets when creating 3D objects?
options: Nets help plan how flat material can be folded into a finished 3D shape
Nets have no practical use
Nets eliminate the need for any measurements
Nets are only used in fiction writing

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Conservation of Biodiversity
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Life Systems strand: biodiversity is the variety of living things in an ecosystem, and conservation efforts aim to protect that variety from threats like habitat loss and pollution.

quiz.q: Biodiversity refers to ___.
options: The variety of living things in an ecosystem
Only the number of trees in a forest
The absence of all living things
A single species living alone

quiz.q: Which is a common threat to biodiversity?
options: Too much protected land
Habitat loss
Nothing threatens biodiversity
Too many conservation programs

quiz.q: Why is biodiversity important to a healthy ecosystem?
options: A variety of species helps maintain balance and resilience in the ecosystem
Biodiversity always harms ecosystems
Biodiversity has no effect on ecosystems
Ecosystems function better with only one species present

quiz.q: Which is an example of a conservation effort to protect biodiversity?
options: Removing all environmental protections
Encouraging unlimited habitat destruction
Ignoring threats to species
Establishing protected wildlife habitats

quiz.q: Why might scientists monitor biodiversity levels over time?
options: To detect changes that might signal environmental problems
Only non-living things are worth monitoring
Monitoring biodiversity serves no purpose
Biodiversity never changes over time

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Relationship with the Commonwealth
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: the Commonwealth is a voluntary association of countries, many once part of the British Empire, that cooperate on shared goals like trade, education, and democracy.

quiz.q: The Commonwealth is best described as ___.
options: A single country
A voluntary association of countries cooperating on shared goals
An organization with no members
A military alliance only

quiz.q: Many Commonwealth countries were historically connected through ___.
options: The Roman Empire
Ancient trade routes only
No historical connection at all
The British Empire

quiz.q: Commonwealth countries often cooperate on issues such as ___.
options: Isolating each other completely
Nothing of shared interest
Trade, education, and democracy
Only military conflict

quiz.q: Is membership in the Commonwealth mandatory for its member countries?
options: Yes, countries are forced to join
No, membership is voluntary
Membership has never been optional
Only some countries are allowed to leave

quiz.q: Why might Canada value its relationship with the Commonwealth?
options: The Commonwealth provides no benefit to Canada
Commonwealth membership isolates Canada from the world
It offers opportunities for cooperation and shared connections with other nations
Canada has no historical ties to Commonwealth countries

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Formal Speech Writing
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Writing strand: a formal speech is organized with a clear introduction, body, and conclusion, uses persuasive or informative language suited to the audience, and is written to be spoken aloud.

quiz.q: A formal speech is typically organized with ___.
options: A single sentence
Only random unrelated points
A clear introduction, body, and conclusion
No clear structure at all

quiz.q: Why should a speech writer consider their audience?
options: Considering the audience makes a speech less effective
All speeches should be written the exact same way regardless of audience
To choose language and content the audience will understand and connect with
The audience never matters when writing a speech

quiz.q: A strong speech introduction often ___.
options: Captures the audience’s attention and introduces the topic
Contains no information at all
Immediately ends the speech
Ignores the topic entirely

quiz.q: Since a speech is meant to be spoken aloud, writers should consider ___.
options: Nothing related to pacing or tone
Making every sentence as long as possible
Only how the words look on paper
How the words will sound and flow when read aloud

quiz.q: A speech’s conclusion should typically ___.
options: Introduce a brand new unrelated topic
Repeat random unrelated facts
Reinforce the main message and leave a lasting impression
Contain no connection to the rest of the speech

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Patterns with Two Variables
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Algebra strand: a pattern with two variables shows how one value changes in relation to another, often organized in a table, where identifying the rule connecting them helps predict future values.

quiz.q: If the rule is y equals x plus 3, what is y when x is 5?
In a table showing two related variables, identifying the rule helps you ___.
options: Only look at one variable
Avoid understanding the relationship
Ignore the pattern entirely
Predict future values

quiz.q: If the rule is y equals x times 2, what is y when x is 7?
If x is 1, 2, 3 and the matching y values are 4, 8, 12, what is the rule connecting x and y?
options: y equals x minus 4
y equals x plus 4
y equals x divided by 4
y equals x times 4

quiz.q: Why are tables useful for exploring patterns with two variables?
options: Tables make patterns impossible to identify
Tables are never used for patterns
Tables only work with a single variable
They organize the data clearly, making the relationship between variables easier to see

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Chemical Changes and Reactions
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Matter and Energy strand: a chemical change occurs when substances react to form new substances with different properties, often shown by signs like colour change, gas bubbles, or heat being released.

quiz.q: A chemical change occurs when ___.
options: Substances react to form new substances with different properties
A substance simply melts
Nothing about the substance changes at all
A substance only changes shape

quiz.q: Which is a common sign that a chemical reaction has occurred?
options: No observable change at all
The object becoming a different shape only
Gas bubbles forming
The object staying exactly the same

quiz.q: Rusting metal is an example of a ___.
options: Chemical change
Change that never actually occurs
Physical change only
Change with no cause

quiz.q: Chemical reactions can release or absorb ___.
options: Only sound
Heat energy
Nothing at all
Only light with no other effect

quiz.q: Why is it useful to be able to identify signs of a chemical reaction?
options: Chemical reactions have no observable signs
Identifying reactions serves no scientific purpose
All changes in matter are identical
It helps distinguish a chemical change from a simple physical change

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Role of the Media in a Democracy
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: media, including news outlets and journalists, plays a role in informing citizens about government actions and current events, which supports an informed and engaged democracy.

quiz.q: One key role of the media in a democracy is to ___.
options: Inform citizens about government actions and current events
Replace the need for elections
Control what citizens are allowed to think
Prevent citizens from learning anything

quiz.q: An informed citizen is better able to ___.
options: Ignore important issues entirely
Participate thoughtfully in democratic decisions like voting
Avoid all civic responsibilities
Make decisions with no available information

quiz.q: Why is it important for media to report accurately?
options: Inaccurate reporting always benefits democracy
Citizens do not need accurate information
Accurate reporting helps citizens make informed decisions
Accuracy has no effect on democracy

quiz.q: Journalists in a democracy often play a role in ___.
options: Investigating and reporting on issues that affect the public
Avoiding any coverage of government actions
Replacing elected officials
Making all government decisions themselves

quiz.q: Why might citizens compare multiple media sources when following an issue?
options: Comparing sources has no value
One source always tells the complete story
Citizens should avoid all media sources
To get a fuller, more balanced understanding of the topic

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Media Literacy: Comparing Media Formats
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Media Literacy strand: different media formats, such as print articles, videos, podcasts, and social media posts, present information differently and can shape how audiences understand a topic.

quiz.q: Which is an example of a media format?
options: A single letter of the alphabet
A blank page
A math equation
A podcast

quiz.q: Different media formats can ___.
options: Present the same topic in different ways
Always present information identically
Have no effect on understanding
Never be compared to each other

quiz.q: A video format might convey information differently from print by using ___.
options: No sound or visuals at all
Visuals, sound, and movement
Only plain text with no other elements
Nothing beyond written words

quiz.q: Why might comparing how different formats cover the same topic be useful?
options: Comparing formats has no value
All formats always present identical information
Only one media format can ever be trusted
It reveals how the choice of format can shape understanding of a topic

quiz.q: Social media posts often present information ___.
options: In short, quickly consumed pieces
In a longer, more detailed form than any other format
Exactly the same way as an academic textbook
With no possibility of bias

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Estimating and Measuring Angles with a Protractor
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Geometry strand: a protractor is a tool used to measure the size of an angle in degrees, and estimating an angle’s size before measuring helps check whether the measurement is reasonable.

quiz.q: A protractor is used to measure ___.
options: The weight of an object
The volume of a shape
The size of an angle in degrees
The length of a line

quiz.q: Before measuring an angle exactly, estimating its size helps you ___.
options: Skip measuring entirely
Avoid using a protractor altogether
Check whether your final measurement is reasonable
Guess randomly with no purpose

quiz.q: An angle that looks close to a right angle is likely close to ___ degrees.
A straight angle measures ___ degrees.
When using a protractor, the vertex of the angle should be lined up with ___.
options: The edge of the paper
The 90-degree mark only
The centre point of the protractor
A random point

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Forces: Friction and Air Resistance
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Structures and Mechanisms strand: friction is a force that resists motion between two surfaces in contact, and air resistance is a similar force that slows objects moving through air.

quiz.q: Friction is a force that ___.
options: Speeds up motion between surfaces
Only exists in outer space
Resists motion between two surfaces in contact
Has no effect on moving objects

quiz.q: Air resistance is a force that ___.
options: Has no connection to motion
Only affects objects underwater
Speeds up objects moving through air with no limit
Slows down objects moving through air

quiz.q: Which surface would likely create more friction?
options: A surface with no contact at all
Friction is unaffected by surface texture
A perfectly smooth, oiled surface
A rough, bumpy surface

quiz.q: Why might a parachute use air resistance to its advantage?
options: Air resistance only affects solid objects, not fabric
Parachutes eliminate air resistance completely
Air resistance has no effect on falling objects
It slows a falling object down for a safer landing

quiz.q: Which situation involves a large amount of friction?
options: Sliding a heavy box across rough pavement
An object floating freely in space
A ball rolling on a frictionless surface
An object at complete rest with no forces acting on it

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Comparing Government Systems: Canada and Other Countries
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies People and Environments strand: comparing Canada’s parliamentary democracy to other government systems, such as a republic or a monarchy without elected representatives, helps highlight different ways countries are governed.

quiz.q: Canada’s system of government is best described as a ___.
options: System with no laws at all
Government led entirely by a single unelected ruler
Parliamentary democracy
Government with no elected officials

quiz.q: Comparing government systems across countries can help students understand ___.
options: That all countries are governed identically
That government systems have no real differences
Different ways nations organize decision-making and representation
That comparing systems serves no purpose

quiz.q: A republic is generally a system where ___.
options: Citizens have no role in choosing leaders
Only a king or queen can ever lead
The head of state is typically elected, not a hereditary monarch
There is no government at all

quiz.q: Why might different countries choose different systems of government?
options: Government systems have no connection to history or culture
Government systems often reflect a country’s unique history, values, and needs
All countries are required to use the same system
Choice of government system is always random

quiz.q: Studying different government systems helps citizens ___.
options: Better understand how their own government compares to others
Conclude that only one system has ever existed
Avoid learning about their own government
Understand that comparison is unnecessary

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Correcting Run-on Sentences and Fragments
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Writing strand: a run-on sentence incorrectly joins two or more complete sentences without proper punctuation, while a sentence fragment is an incomplete sentence missing a subject, verb, or complete thought.

quiz.q: A run-on sentence is one that ___.
options: Has perfect punctuation throughout
Is always too short
Incorrectly joins two or more complete sentences without proper punctuation
Contains no subject or verb at all

quiz.q: A sentence fragment is ___.
options: A complete sentence with excess punctuation
A sentence with no possible errors
Always longer than a run-on sentence
An incomplete sentence missing a subject, verb, or complete thought

quiz.q: Which of these is a sentence fragment?
options: The dog ran down the street.
Running down the street.
She smiled.
The dog ran down the street, and it barked loudly.

quiz.q: Which of these is a run-on sentence?
options: I like pizza.
I like pizza, and I also like pasta.
I like pizza I also like pasta.
Pizza is my favourite food.

quiz.q: How can a run-on sentence often be corrected?
options: Run-on sentences cannot be corrected
By adding proper punctuation or splitting it into separate sentences
By removing all punctuation entirely
By making the sentence even longer

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Comparing Costs and Value for Money
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Financial Literacy strand: comparing costs and value for money involves looking beyond price alone to consider quality, quantity, and unit rates when deciding which option offers the best value.

quiz.q: Value for money means considering ___.
options: Nothing beyond the item’s colour
Only the lowest price, regardless of quality
Only the brand name
Price along with quality and quantity

quiz.q: If a 500 mL bottle costs $2 and a 1 L bottle costs $3.50, which offers better value per litre?
options: They offer identical value
Value cannot be compared here
The 1 L bottle
The 500 mL bottle

quiz.q: Why might a shopper compare unit rates when choosing between products?
options: Unit rates have no connection to value
Comparing unit rates always wastes time
Price should never be considered when shopping
To determine which option provides more value for the price

quiz.q: Which factor, beyond price, might affect a product’s overall value?
options: Nothing beyond the sticker price
The day of the week it was purchased
Its quality and how long it lasts
The store’s wall colour

quiz.q: A shopper choosing the cheapest option without considering quality might end up with ___.
options: The best possible value automatically
No possible downsides
A product that does not meet their needs despite the lower price
Guaranteed excellent value every time

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Fossils and What They Tell Us About Earth’s History
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: fossils are the preserved remains or traces of ancient organisms, and studying them helps scientists understand how life and environments on Earth have changed over time.

quiz.q: A fossil is best described as ___.
options: A man-made object
The preserved remains or traces of an ancient organism
A living organism found today
A type of modern rock with no history

quiz.q: Studying fossils helps scientists understand ___.
options: Only modern technology
Only current weather patterns
How life and environments on Earth have changed over time
Nothing about Earth’s history

quiz.q: Fossils are often found preserved within ___.
options: Layers of sedimentary rock
Modern plastic materials
Clouds
Only living trees

quiz.q: Why might finding a fossil of a sea creature high in the mountains be significant?
options: It could suggest that area was once covered by water long ago
It has no scientific significance at all
It proves mountains never change over time
It means sea creatures currently live in the mountains

quiz.q: Scientists who study fossils are called ___.
options: Meteorologists
Paleontologists
Astronomers
Chemists

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Truth and Reconciliation Commission
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 5 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: the Truth and Reconciliation Commission of Canada was established to document the history and impact of the residential school system and to make recommendations toward healing and reconciliation.

quiz.q: The Truth and Reconciliation Commission was established to document the history of ___.
options: The Great Depression
The residential school system
The building of the railway
The War of 1812

quiz.q: One purpose of the Truth and Reconciliation Commission was to ___.
options: Prevent any future discussion of this history
Ignore the experiences of residential school survivors
Erase historical records completely
Make recommendations toward healing and reconciliation

quiz.q: The Truth and Reconciliation Commission gathered information partly by ___.
options: Only using fictional accounts
